content.question: If early tracking were eliminated, would segregation patterns driven by free school choice and catchment-area rules persist, increase, or decrease? Could non-tracked schools recreate within-school segregation through streaming or elective grouping?
content.reduction_path: Study education systems that eliminated tracking but retained school choice (e.g., parts of Germany, Nordic countries); conduct comparative case analysis of within-school stratification in non-tracked systems; survey school-level implementation data on streaming and advanced/remedial grouping to model degree of de facto re-segregation risk
